skill.name: dependency-security
skill.description: Guide dependency vulnerability checks, safe package updates, lockfile hygiene, and SBOM generation for JavaScript projects.
skill.summary: Run structured dependency security checks and produce SBOM guidance for npm-based projects.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution findings are mostly false positives from Markdown command examples, not embedded executable code. The skill is still medium risk because it explicitly instructs agents to run npm and npx commands that may download packages, modify dependency files, and contact package registries. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Agent May Run Package Manager Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends npm, npx, git, and CI commands. These are legitimate for dependency security work, but they can alter project files, run package lifecycle behavior, or execute downloaded tools if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and relevant to the skill purpose. Risk depends on operator approval and environment controls, so malicious intent is not confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Dependency Updates Can Change Application Behavior
medium_findings.description: The workflow includes npm audit fix, npm audit fix --force, npm-check-updates, and package-lock commits. These actions can introduce breaking changes or unexpected dependency graph changes if applied automatically.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed commands explicitly update dependencies and write generated files. The skill warns about force updates, which reduces concern but does not remove operational risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Registry URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to npm, OWASP, CycloneDX, and npm documentation resources. They are expected references for this skill and show no evidence of exfiltration or suspicious endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible, standard project or registry references. No code sends secrets or project data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Static Sensitive File Finding Is Benign
low_findings.description: The .npmrc references describe safe registry configuration and a checklist item. No authentication token, secret value, or instruction to read a user's private .npmrc file was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain configuration examples only. There is no credential material or credential access pattern.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The analyzer combined command examples, public URLs, and .npmrc references into a critical heuristic. Review found documentation and workflow guidance only, with no credential access, exfiltration endpoint, or obfuscated behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are visible documentation examples and public references. No hidden code path or instruction to collect secrets was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The description discusses dependency security and SBOM generation. No weak cryptographic algorithm usage or cryptographic implementation was found in the cited metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain descriptive metadata. It contains no cryptographic API, hash algorithm, or security implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Tool Execution Through npx
dangerous_patterns.description: Several examples use npx to run package-supplied tools. This is common for npm audits and SBOM work, but it can execute code from packages resolved from a registry.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit and technically capable of executing package code. The context is legitimate security tooling, so this is a controlled-use concern rather than confirmed abuse.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Substitution in Output Filename
dangerous_patterns.description: The example uses command substitution to add a date to an output filename. The expression is static and not user-controlled, so command injection evidence was not found.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell substitution is visible and fixed to a date command. No untrusted input is interpolated into the command.

content.value_statement: Dependency changes can introduce vulnerable packages, stale lockfiles, and unclear software inventories. This skill provides a repeatable checklist for npm audits, safer updates, and SBOM generation.
actual_capabilities: Guides npm audit checks for high and critical vulnerability gates.
Recommends safe dependency update steps using patch and minor targets.
Provides CycloneDX and SPDX SBOM generation commands.
Shows GitHub Actions examples for dependency audits and SBOM artifacts.
Defines checklists for new projects, new dependencies, and regular reviews.
limitations: Focused on npm and JavaScript dependency workflows.
Does not verify vulnerability data without running external tools.
Some recommended commands can modify files or download packages.
Does not replace manual review of breaking changes and licenses.

use_cases.title: Review a new npm package
use_cases.description: Check package reputation, license fit, audit results, and lockfile changes before merging.

use_cases.title: Add dependency checks to CI
use_cases.description: Create a pipeline step that fails builds on high or critical dependency vulnerabilities.

use_cases.title: Prepare an SBOM for compliance
use_cases.description: Generate a software bill of materials and store it as a build artifact.

prompt_templates.title: Check a package change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review before adding a dependency
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this package change for dependency security. Check audit risk, package maintenance, license concerns, and lockfile impact.

prompt_templates.title: Plan safe updates
prompt_templates.scenario: Patch and minor dependency update planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a safe dependency update plan for this npm project. Separate patch, minor, and major updates, and include test steps.

prompt_templates.title: Add CI security gates
prompt_templates.prompt: Add dependency security checks to CI. Use npm audit with a high severity gate and include an SBOM artifact step.

prompt_templates.title: Perform supply chain review
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a supply chain security review for this release. Cover vulnerabilities, outdated packages, lockfile integrity, SBOM status, and risky transitive dependencies.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that adds three npm dependencies.
output_examples.output: A dependency risk summary with audit status, package maintenance notes, license concerns, and required follow-up actions.

output_examples.input: Prepare CI dependency security checks for a Node.js repository.
output_examples.output: A concise pipeline plan that installs with npm ci, blocks high severity findings, and uploads an SBOM artifact.

output_examples.input: Run a monthly dependency security review.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review report covering vulnerable packages, stale dependencies, SBOM freshness, and safe update order.

best_practices: Review commands before execution, especially npm audit fix --force and npx tools.
Commit lockfiles and use npm ci in CI for reproducible dependency installs.
Generate SBOM files during release builds and store them with build artifacts.
anti_patterns: Running automatic dependency fixes without tests or code review.
Ignoring lockfile changes after adding or updating dependencies.
Treating SBOM generation as a substitute for vulnerability triage.

faq.question: Does this skill run scans by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides commands and workflow guidance that an agent or user may run.

faq.question: Which ecosystem does it target?
faq.answer: It mainly targets npm and JavaScript projects, with examples for GitHub Actions.

faq.question: Can it generate an SBOM?
faq.answer: It provides CycloneDX and SPDX command examples for generating SBOM files.

faq.question: Is it safe to use in production repositories?
faq.answer: Use it with review. Some commands can update dependencies, change lockfiles, or download tools.

faq.question: Does it handle private registry credentials?
faq.answer: No. The skill shows basic .npmrc safety settings and does not request or process tokens.

faq.question: Can it replace a security engineer review?
faq.answer: No. It supports dependency triage, but humans should review breaking changes and risk acceptance.
